steps.title: Identify where the descriptive clause must go
steps.content: The key signal is the clause “which are skeletal muscles.” When a clause describes a noun and is not essential to the main meaning of the sentence, it must appear right after that noun and be enclosed by commas on both sides.

steps.title: Apply the rule to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C places the clause immediately after the subject with commas on both sides, then continues with the main predicates “are cylindrical in shape and help move the fibula and humerus, respectively.” The other options either separate this clause from what it describes, fail to use paired commas properly, or break up phrases.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
